description_long: This research institute is a large, multi-level building with a modern, rectilinear design, set atop a contoured terrain base. The structure features a mix of white, brown, and black exterior surfaces, with flat roofs and extensive use of horizontal and vertical lines. Large window panels and open terraces are visible, suggesting ample natural light and outdoor connectivity. The building is surrounded by landscaped grounds, with pathways and retaining walls integrated into the sloped site. The overall form is characterized by stacked volumes and cantilevered sections, creating dynamic overhangs and shaded areas. The design emphasizes functionality, accessibility, and a contemporary aesthetic suitable for advanced research activities.
description: A modern, multi-level research institute with flat roofs, stacked volumes, and a mix of white, brown, and black surfaces, set on landscaped, sloped terrain.
description_view_0: This view highlights the building's layered structure, with prominent brown and white sections and a visible terrace on the left side.
description_view_1: From this angle, the main entrance area and a large windowed section are visible, along with the integration of the building into the sloped terrain.
description_view_2: This side shows the rear elevation, featuring a long, flat roof and a combination of white and brown facades, as well as a retaining wall.
description_view_3: This perspective reveals the building's side elevation, with a focus on the white and brown exterior walls and the landscaped base.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is broad and integrated into the lower level, featuring a flat overhang for weather protection. The entrance is likely accessed via a short, wide pathway or steps, with large glass doors and adjacent window panels. The design is modern and accessible, with no visible decorative elements or signage.
